title: Real Madrid 1-2 Bayern Munich: Kane Strikes as Bayern Snatch First-Leg Advantage at the Bernabéu
excerpt: Harry Kane returned from injury to score and Bayern Munich claimed a crucial 2-1 win at the Bernábéu — their first victory on Real Madrid's turf in 25 years — in the UCL quarter-final first leg.
content:
## A Historic Night in Madrid

The Santiago Bernébu has been Bayern Munich’s graveyard for a quarter of a century. Tuesday night finally changed that. In front of 77,106 spectators, the Bavarians produced a composed, clinical performance to win 2-1 and carry a valuable advantage back to the Fußball Arena München for the second leg on 15 April.

It was Bayern’s first victory at the Bernébu since 2001, and their first win over Real Madrid in any competition in ten attempts.

## Díaz Opens the Scoring Late in the First Half

The first half was tightly contested with both sides probing without finding a way through in the opening exchanges. The breakthrough came in the 41st minute. Harry Kane — playing despite an ankle injury — was central to the build-up before the ball was threaded through to Luis Díaz inside the area. The Colombia winger took one touch to steady himself and drove a low shot past Andriy Lunin into the far corner.

Bayern went into the interval with a one-goal lead and the priceless knowledge that they had controlled the opening chapter on hostile territory.

## Kane Doubles the Advantage Within Seconds of the Restart

Just 20 seconds after the restart — the teams barely settled from the kickoff — Kane received the ball at the edge of the box and unleashed a low, first-time strike that arrowed into the corner past a wrong-footed Lunin.

The timing of the goal was devastating. It was Kane’s 49th goal of the season across all competitions — a figure that underlines why Bayern made him their centrepiece this campaign.

## Mbappé Gives Madrid a Lifeline

Real Madrid did not fold. Mbappé provided a response in the 74th minute. Trent Alexander-Arnold picked him out with a precise low cross from the right, and the Frenchman converted from close range to make it 2-1.

It was Mbappé’s 20th Champions League goal since the start of last season. Manuel Neuer was called upon repeatedly in a frantic closing period, finishing the evening with nine saves.

## What the Result Means Ahead of the Second Leg

Bayern travel to Munich holding a one-goal advantage with an away goal to their name. History backs them: the Bavarians have advanced in 12 of the 13 Champions League ties in which they have won the first leg away from home.

For Real Madrid, the task is clear — win in Munich by at least two goals without reply to advance. Ancelotti’s side have orchestrated remarkable comeback nights before. This squad understands better than most that a single away goal can completely reshape a knockout tie. The second leg promises everything.
